sourceMovie.title: Pig
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Pig (2021) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Michael Sarnoski's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Premonition
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pig clicked for you, Premonition (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pig?
faq.answer: Try Premonition, Greta, Remember—each shares DNA with Pig while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pig?
faq.answer: Pig (2021) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pig worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
